leaf_entry_definition: SHA-256 digest of the complete Signed Statement (COSE_Sign1) bytes, hex-encoded
tree_construction: tree_size=8; statement digest at leaf_index=2; filler leaf i = SHA-256('scitt-cose test vectors v1 :: <vector-id> :: filler leaf <i>'); leaves in index order; RFC 9162 SHA-256 tree

vectors.description: Happy path: EdDSA Signed Statement + RFC9162_SHA256 receipt; everything verifies.
Happy path: ES256 Signed Statement + RFC9162_SHA256 receipt; everything verifies.
Inclusion path with one flipped byte (first audit-path node). The reconstructed root no longer matches what the log signed; a verifier MUST reject. Statement is honest — the failure is receipt-only.

Receipt whose protected vds (label 395) is 2, not 1 (RFC9162_SHA256). Proof and signature are otherwise honest, so the ONLY conformant rejection reason is the unsupported verifiable data structure — read from the protected header, never the unprotected one.
Signed Statement whose signature byte was flipped. The receipt is minted over the digest of the TAMPERED bytes and verifies — isolating the failure to the statement signature alone.

Happy path: real pyscitt did:x509 Signed Statement (ES256, P-256) + real CCF ccf.v1 Merkle receipt (vds=2, ES384, P-384) from scitt-ccf-ledger v7.0.6. Captured 2026-06-26 against a local VIRTUAL-mode CCF node.
